name: Discovery endpoint
outcome: A relying party can fetch .well-known/openid-configuration and learn the server's OIDC capabilities and endpoint URLs.
scope: Add the discovery document builder and the AuthorizationServer.openidConfiguration() method.
acceptance_criteria: openidConfiguration() returns a well-formed document with issuer, endpoint URLs, and auto-derived capability fields
A consumer metadata override shallow-merges into the generated document
steps: src/oidc — add a discovery document builder that auto-derives response_types_supported, grant_types_supported, id_token_signing_alg_values_supported, code_challenge_methods_supported, subject_types_supported and token_endpoint_auth_methods_supported

discovery builder — set scopes_supported to the standard OIDC scopes the library recognizes (openid, profile, email, address, phone, offline_access); the library has no scope registry, so any non-standard scopes must be supplied by the consumer via the metadata override
discovery builder — include the consumer-supplied endpoint URLs and shallow-merge the optional metadata override
AuthorizationServer — add an openidConfiguration() method returning the document as a ResponseInterface
test/unit — cover auto-derived fields, the code-only and public-subject constraints, and the metadata merge
notes: The library does not own routing, so endpoint URLs are supplied explicitly by the consumer. issuer is reused from options. scopes_supported cannot be truly auto-derived — the library has no scope registry — so it lists only the standard OIDC scopes; consumer-specific scopes come via the metadata override. claims_supported is likewise unknowable by the library and only appears if the consumer provides it via the metadata override.
